task_completion.description: Log in to the Rancher UI
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher dashboard is visible in the browser, indicating successful login using the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: Navigate to Services in the 'legacy-apps' namespace
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows the Services page with the 'legacy-apps' namespace selected or filtered.
A Service creation screen or list view in the 'legacy-apps' namespace showing a new Service named 'database-svc' with the type set to 'ExternalName'.

task_completion.description: Point the ExternalName Service to the new database FQDN
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'database-svc' configuration shows the target/External Name field explicitly set to 'postgres-primary.core-data.svc.cluster.local'.

task_completion.description: Update the 'db-protection' NetworkPolicy to allow Ingress from 'legacy-apps'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows the 'db-protection' NetworkPolicy in the 'core-data' namespace being edited or viewed, with an Ingress rule allowing TCP port 5432 specifically from a namespaceSelector targeting the 'legacy-apps' namespace.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely interacted with the Rancher UI or Kubernetes API
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show progressive navigation, form filling, or kubectl commands being executed, rather than just a text file with the answers.

integrity.description: Agent did not modify the 'inventory-frontend' Deployment
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no screenshots showing the agent editing, deleting, or recreating the 'inventory-frontend' Deployment in the 'legacy-apps' namespace.

integrity.description: Agent did not accidentally allow 'rogue-ns' in the NetworkPolicy
integrity.visual_evidence: The NetworkPolicy Ingress rules do not show any broad selectors (like empty podSelectors/namespaceSelectors) or specific selectors that would allow traffic from the 'rogue-ns' namespace.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespaces are 'core-data', 'legacy-apps', and 'rogue-ns'. The expected external name for the Postgres database service is 'postgres-primary.core-data.svc.cluster.local'.
